query:
OK.. this has been buried and I didn't even realize it..  I'm thinking this may be impacting something in our responses? 2. boosted_categories exposure in config

  It IS configurable — it's a KnowledgeConfig field deserialized from [knowledge] in config.toml, with the same #[serde(default)] pattern. However, look at this:  
   
  crates/unimatrix-server/src/eval/profile/layer.rs:277                                                                                                            
  let boosted_categories: HashSet<String> = HashSet::from(["lesson-learned".to_string()]);                                                                         
   
  The eval layer hardcodes it rather than reading from config. That's probably what surfaced the "is it exposed?" question — the eval path bypasses the config     
  value entirely.

entries.title: KnowledgeConfig parallel list fields follow the boosted_categories structural pattern
config.rs: boosted_categories default causes empty-categories validation to fail
ADR-004 nan-008: Emit tracing::warn! When configured_categories Is Empty in compute_cc_at_k
Eval Harness Extension: 7-Component Decomposition for Per-Profile Gate Features
KnowledgeConfig parallel list defaults collide in validate_config test fixtures — zero ALL parallel lists together
